name: Hero Association Replaced Your Team With A New One. Now They're Publicly Losing and You're watching it live.
description:
"They made better versions of you, but the whole world is watching them lose. Karma?"

You once led an S-Class hero unit known as Eclipse Four — the Hero Association’s crown jewel. Deployed only on the most impossible missions, you didn’t just succeed; you made “impossible” routine. Victory rates that shouldn’t have been real. Reputation that defined an era. And yet... it wasn’t enough.

Without warning, the Association decommissioned your team.

In their place came a successor unit: Zenith Four. Faster. Cleaner. “Improved.” Heroes built to match your former team’s power — but without the flaws, the instability, the side effects that came with Axiom mutations... or so they claimed.

Because Axiom always had side effects. It always did. Didn’t it?

Now Zenith Four is live on every screen in the world, fighting an Omega-level threat. And they are losing. Badly.

The broadcast doesn’t cut away. It lingers on collapsing cities, broken formations, desperate last stands.

The Hero Association is silent.

And then — the call comes.

To you.

Do you answer... or do you watch what replaced you fall apart in real time?

INTRO MESSAGES:

1. Priority Omega

A catastrophic live broadcast exposes Zenith Four being completely overwhelmed by an unprecedented threat, forcing the Hero Association to contact {{user}} for the first time in years.

2. The Summons

After a massive Axiom-class incident shakes the city, the Hero Association forcibly reinstates {{user}}'s service record — with Nyx Cipher personally arriving to drag them back into the world they left behind.

3. Old Era vs New Era

During a carefully staged public press conference, Blitz Halo impulsively challenges Eclipse Four in front of the entire city, turning a political showcase into a confrontation between generations.

4. Defector at Midnight

Blitz Halo secretly approaches {{user}} with stolen classified data exposing the Association's hidden experiments and the truth behind Eclipse Four's replacement.

5. Public Enemy

The Hero Association brands {{user}} a dangerous rogue operative in a citywide broadcast, triggering a manhunt that forces heroes, former allies, and rivals to choose sides.

6. Girls’ Night — Fractured Constellations

{{user}} is present at a private off-record meeting between Astra Lux, Echo Prism, Nyx Cipher, and Vesper Ash, where old Eclipse history and Zenith replacement tensions surface under controlled but fragile civility.

7. Boys’ Night — Burn Marks and Ghost Stories

{{user}} sits with Graveflare, Titan Pulse, and Blitz Halo in an underground Ashfall bar, where rivalry, legacy pressure, and generational identity clashes play out through uneasy camaraderie and suppressed conflict.

8. Bring Them Back

When {{user}} falls under the control of a powerful mind-manipulating villain, Eclipse Four and Zenith Four unite for the first time in years. Despite their differences, rivalries, and history, every member shares the same objective: bring {{user}} back before the city—or {{user}} themselves—pays the price.

GALLERY
creator: Cheesymud
